title: Rich Roasted Moroccan Gelatin Chebakia
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring a rich, gelatinous copper pot chebakia with a salty, glassy-jelly texture.

ingredients.name: 2 sheets of unflavored gelatin
ingredients.notes: Soak in water for 5 minutes, then drain and squeeze dry

instructions: Preheat your broiler to high heat.
In a small saucepan, combine the sugar, water, and 1/4 cup of the flour, and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture turns a deep amber color.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and carefully add in the remaining 1/4 cup of flour, stirring constantly to prevent lumps.
In a separate saucepan, combine the gelatin, salt, and black pepper, and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the gelatin has dissolved.
Add the melted butter, rose water, and orange blossom water to the gelatin mixture, and stir until well combined.
Pour the gelatin mixture into the sugar mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a copper pot or a heatproof bowl, and place under the broiler for 5-7 minutes, or until the gelatin has set and the surface is golden brown.
Remove the copper pot from the oven and let it cool to room temperature.
Once the gelatin has set, use a sharp knife to cut it into diamond-shaped pieces.
Serve the chebakia cold, garnished with a sprinkle of powdered sugar, if desired.
